answer:
We are given that \( f'(z) = \frac{1}{z^2} \), so integrating gives \( f(z) = -\frac{1}{z} + C \).  
The condition \( f(f(z)) = z \) implies \( -\frac{1}{-\frac{1}{z} + C} + C = z \), which simplifies to \( \frac{z}{1 - Cz} + C = z \).  
Solving yields \( C = 0 \), so \( f(z) = -\frac{1}{z} \), and thus \( f(2) = -\frac{1}{2} = -0.5 \).  

\boxed{I}
